creator: Alexander Terp
description:
All case expressions in a switch are evaluated before matching, including those after the matching case. Functions with side effects in case expressions will execute for every case.

**Repro:**
```
fn log(x):
    print("evaluating: {x}")
    return x

switch 1:
    case log(1):
        print("matched 1")
    case log(2):
        print("matched 2")
// Output includes "evaluating: 1" AND "evaluating: 2"
// plus if multiple cases match, error RAD20035
```

Most switch implementations use lazy evaluation (stop after first match).

**Test files:** `bugs/control_flow/54_switch_case_side_effects.rad`
title: Switch evaluates ALL case expressions eagerly, even after match found
